root_cause_addressed.detail: Root cause: resolve_credential('anthropic') had a legacy-env branch that read the bare CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN env var as an INDEPENDENT credential source, evaluated AFTER the DB-row check but BEFORE any 'no credential' honesty check. On this VM the repo-root .env carries a real (test-fixture) oat01 token; when that value is present in the ambient process environment, it leaks into resolve_credential regardless of whether: (a) the DB row for that credential was deliberately deleted by the operator (resurrects a removed credential), or (b) the call was for a DIFFERENT provider path that should have raised honestly (cross-provider risk via a claude-* model call when only OpenRouter creds exist, since the anthropic env fallback still fired internally when probed). The fix deletes the env-read branch entirely, making the encrypted DB row (checked first, unconditionally, on every single resolve_credential call - not cached, not restart-dependent) the sole source of truth for an oauth_token credential. This is a root-cause code-logic fix (removing a code path), not a test change or a workaround.
code_refs: apps/api/app/services/llm_client.py:441-458 (diff region) - the 'oat = os.environ.get("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN"); if oat: return ...' branch is deleted outright and replaced with an explanatory NOTE comment; the surrounding AETHER_LLM_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env fallback logic (api_key path) is byte-for-byte unchanged below it.

cross_check.no_cross_provider_fallback: Confirmed intact - TestNoCrossProviderFallback (4 tests, test_provider_config.py) passed in the same run; independently re-verified 2 of its members were among the 3 originally-failing node-ids and now pass under the exact leak condition (see targeted_tests_result).
cross_check.subscription_oauth_still_rejected: Confirmed - test_preexisting_subscription_oauth_credential_not_resolved (test_gap_p5_auth_compliance.py) passed; independently re-verified this was one of the 3 originally-failing node-ids and now passes under the exact leak condition.
cross_check.defect_a_dual_mode_intact: Confirmed - test_gap_p7_def_a_dual_mode.py (10 tests) and test_gap_d1_dual_auth.py (10 tests) both fully green in the same run; the DB-row path for BOTH api_key and oauth_token auth modes (the '1. Encrypted DB credential ... wins' block, lines above the diff) is untouched by this diff.
cross_check.no_cross_provider_env_read_added: Confirmed the deleted branch was anthropic-scoped only; the openrouter branch further down the function (also unchanged, outside the diff) was never touched.

no_test_weakened.evidence: `git show 952828f --stat` shows exactly one file changed: apps/api/app/services/llm_client.py (+14/-8). No test file appears in the diff at all - this is fix-in-logic, not fix-by-editing-tests. Diffed test_provider_config.py, test_gap_p5_auth_compliance.py, test_gap_p7_def_a_dual_mode.py, test_gap_d1_dual_auth.py against main/parent: byte-identical (not part of the commit).

targeted_tests_result.command: cd apps/api && DATABASE_URL="$DATABASE_URL_TEST" DATABASE_URL_TEST="$DATABASE_URL_TEST" AETHER_ASYNC_GENERATION=false flock /tmp/aether-pytest.lock python3 -m pytest tests/test_provider_config.py tests/test_gap_p5_auth_compliance.py tests/test_gap_p7_def_a_dual_mode.py tests/test_gap_d1_dual_auth.py -q
targeted_tests_result.postfix_clean_shell_result: 58 passed, 77 warnings in 76.57s [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE, this run, worktree agent-a14673172925bd1dc @ 952828f] - note: matches fixer's claimed '48+10' (48 in the 3-file suite + 10 in test_gap_d1_dual_auth.py).
targeted_tests_result.adversarial_leak_reproduction: The clean-shell run above does NOT by itself prove the fix works, since CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN was not ambient in that shell either. To adversarially confirm the fix under the ACTUAL failure condition, independently: (1) built a detached worktree at the parent commit (952828f^ == c091e79, pre-fix code), (2) exported the real CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN value read from the repo-root .env (a test-fixture token, prefix 'sk-ant-oat01...', confirmed NOT a live secret - value contains the literal substring 'MVagentsPAIDretest-fake' followed by zero-padding) into the pytest process environment, (3) re-ran the 4-file suite. RESULT: exactly 3 failed, 55 passed - FAILED: test_provider_config.py::TestNoCrossProviderFallback::test_anthropic_model_with_only_openrouter_creds_raises, test_provider_config.py::TestNoCrossProviderFallback::test_call_live_never_routes_anthropic_traffic_to_openrouter, test_gap_p5_auth_compliance.py::test_preexisting_subscription_oauth_credential_not_resolved. This exactly matches the fixer's own baseline evidence log (uat evidence dir, untracked, found in the fix worktree: baseline-3tests-20260718T031002Z.log) and the commit message's '3 failed' claim. (4) Re-ran the SAME 3 node-ids against the FIXED commit (952828f) WITH the identical leaked env var still exported: 5 passed (0 failed) - the fix removes the failure under the exact real-world leak condition, not merely in a sanitized test shell.
targeted_tests_result.conclusion: Both fail-before and pass-after are independently and freshly reproduced under the actual defect condition (ambient env leak), not just re-stated from the fixer's report.

prohibited_patterns_scan_method: grep -inE 'TODO|FIXME|eslint-disable|@ts-ignore|as any|Math\.random|--no-verify|placeholder|mock data|hack|xxx' over `git diff 952828f^ 952828f` - zero matches. No suppressed exceptions introduced (diff is a pure deletion of one branch plus a NOTE comment).

sanity_worker_and_restart.question: Does removing the env-branch break the operator's legitimately-pasted oat01 token flow (restart-survival / async worker)?
sanity_worker_and_restart.assessment: NO - verified by reading the actual call graph, not just inferring from comments.
evidence: The DB write (ProviderCredentialRepository().upsert(...), apps/api/app/routers/agents.py ~L1953) happens and is durably committed BEFORE the best-effort .env sync (env_file_writer.sync_oauth_token_env, explicitly documented in-code as 'a sync failure does not fail the save'). So by the time a save-credential request returns 200, the DB row already exists - there is no race window where only the env var would carry a fresher secret.

grep across apps/api/app for CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN shows exactly 2 non-comment reference sites: env_file_writer.py (the WRITE side, sync-only, unaffected) and the deleted llm_client.py READ branch. No other code path (worker, health check, admin UI) reads this env var directly.

The async worker (apps/api/app/workers/tasks.py) imports app.routers.agents._pipeline_core, which resolves credentials via resolve_user_credential -> resolve_credential - i.e. the SAME DB-first resolution path the API request handler uses, on every job execution (not cached from process start, not read from its own env). The worker therefore never depended on the env var for its own resolution to begin with; removing the env-branch does not change worker behavior.

Restart-survival is inherently satisfied by the DB row persisting in Postgres across process restarts - resolve_credential does a fresh DB lookup on every call regardless of process age, so no env-var re-hydration is needed for this resolver's own correctness.

reasons: Deletes exactly the vulnerable code path (env-branch), does not touch the DB-first resolution, api_key env fallback, subscription_oauth rejection, or openrouter resolution - a minimal, targeted diff (+14/-8 in one file).

Independently reproduced fail-before (3 failed) and pass-after (0 failed) under the REAL leak condition (ambient CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN set from the actual .env value), not just in a clean shell that happens to lack the variable - this is a materially stronger verification than simply re-running the fixer's reported commands.
No test files touched at all in this commit - fix is unambiguously in production logic.

Verified via source-reading (not assumption) that the async worker and restart-survival concerns raised in the task are non-issues: both resolve via the DB row on every call, and DB-write-before-env-write ordering removes any race.
concerns: docs/delivery/PHASE7-DEFECT-A-BLUEPRINT.md (the ORIGINALLY-APPROVED architecture doc for this credential path, sections 3.3/5.2/14.1/229) explicitly specified adding this exact env-branch, citing '(a) the async worker (ASYNC-001) and (b) surviving restarts' as its rationale, and explicitly deferred real async-worker consumption design to a separate gap (GAP-P7-ASYNC-001, 'not here'). This fix reverses that approved design point. Verified this reversal is SAFe in practice (see sanity_worker_and_restart), and the reversal is directly evidenced by 3 concrete adversarially-discovered test failures the original design did not anticipate, which is the normal/expected way a later MV finding supersedes an earlier architecture decision. Recommend, as a non-blocking follow-up, that PHASE7-DEFECT-A-BLUEPRINT.md be annotated (not this fix's job) to mark section 5.2's env-branch guidance as superseded by MV-system-002 so a future reader does not reintroduce it.

env_file_writer.py's module docstring (line 6-9, unchanged by this diff) still says the .env sync exists 'so the async worker and a process restart can source it' - now stale/misleading given resolve_credential no longer reads it back for either purpose. Cosmetic/documentation drift only, does not affect runtime correctness, but should be corrected in a follow-up doc pass so the comment does not contradict the code it sits next to.
